summary: Closed Codex #20875/DCG sibling tracking after current-state re-evaluation: upstream remains closed, Joshua's sibling comment exists, local DCG doctrine remains necessary, and the volatile /tmp receipt is superseded by canonical evidence.

decision.reason: Codex #20875 was closed as an internal fork fix rather than public upstream behavior, and Flywheel's local DCG prose-trigger class remains orthogonal: DCG still protects shell prose in bead/dispatch bodies. The local structural gate now catches and routes that class directly.

evidence.result: issue CLOSED at 2026-05-03T19:59:15Z; JYeswak sibling evidence comment present at 2026-05-04T10:58:28Z

evidence.command: .flywheel/scripts/memory-rule-gate-parity-detector.sh check --json | jq '{remaining: ((.partial_rules + .unwired_rules) | map(select(.memory_path | endswith("feedback_dcg_prose_trigger_strip_dangerous_substrings.md"))) | length), wired, partial, unwired, total_meta_rules}'

did: Verified current upstream issue state and Joshua sibling evidence comment.
